mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 195, equity risk premiums expand due to sustained monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.0% and CPIAUCSL at 197.8 suggest tightening regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.0% is at the trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE > 5.0 are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1800 thousand units, indicating weakening aggregate demand that compresses equity valuations and produces negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1800 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 353866M and HOUST at 2014K suggest demand resilience
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between RSAFS-HOUST joint weakness and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands above 109.0 (index 2017=100) while remaining below 110.0, signaling moderate supply-side growth that supports earnings without triggering inflation concerns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 108.96 is just below the trigger threshold
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO is in the 109-110 range, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand new jobs per month while UNRATE remains stable, indicating labor market softening that precedes broader economic weakness and produces negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100 thousand AND UNRATE stable (within ±0.1pp)
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 134289K suggests ongoing employment strength
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal of slowing payrolls with stable unemployment fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 2100 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month, signaling robust consumer and residential demand that supports equity valuations and produces positive 5-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 2100 thousand units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 2014K is below threshold; RSAFS at 353866M
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between HOUST and RSAFS growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains at or above 4.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 197, indicating a restrictive monetary stance with disinflation progress that supports equity valuations through lower terminal rate expectations, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.0% and CPIAUCSL at 197.8 near inflection point
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window in disinflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, signaling simultaneous supply contraction and labor market weakness that indicates recession risk and produces negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 108.96 and UNRATE at 5.0% require monitoring for deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal of declining production and rising unemployment fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC > 0.03.
